name: vtex
description: Fig autocomplete for VTEX IO's CLI - VTEX IO's CLI allows you to perform any action necessary to your development process, such as linking local files to the VTEX platform, managing workspaces, and releasing new app versions

subcommands.name: add
subcommands.description: Adds the specified app(s) to the manifest's dependencies

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) of the dependency to include in the manifest.json file

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) of the multiple dependencies to include in the manifest.json file

subcommands.name: autoupdate
subcommands.description: Automatically updates VTEX IO's CLI

args.name: CHANNEL
args.description: Channel to automatically updates

subcommands.name: browse
subcommands.description: Opens the URL relative to your current workspace and account in a new browser window

name: --qr
-q
options.description: Prints a QR Code on the terminal

args.name: PATH
args.description: Relative path from https://{workspace}--{account}.myvtex.com/

subcommands.name: config
subcommands.description: Env Configuration

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Prints the value of the requested configuration key

args.name: CONFIGNAME
args.description: Configuration to retrieve the value from

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Resets the specified configuration to its default value

args.name: CONFIGNAME
args.description: Name of the configuration to reset

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets the value of a configuration key

args.name: CONFIGNAME
args.description: Name of the configuration to reset

args.name: VALUE
args.description: New value of the specified configuration

subcommands.name: debug dotnet
subcommands.description: Debug .NET applications (IDEs only)

args.name: DEBUGINST
args.description: Name of the .NET application to debug

subcommands.name: deploy
subcommands.description: Publishes an app as a stable version. Only works for apps previously published as a release candidate version [see vtex publish --help]

name: --force
-f
options.description: (Use with caution.) Ignores the testing period of 7 minutes after publishing an app

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app you want to deploy

subcommands.name: deprecate
subcommands.description: Deprecates the specified app, uninstalling and downgrading it to the latest stable version in every VTEX account

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to deprecate

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to deprecate

subcommands.name: deps
subcommands.description: Displays the differences between the dependencies of two distinct workspaces. If a single parameter is passed, the specified workspace's dependencies are compared with the master's. If no parameter is passed, the diff is made between the current workspace and master

subcommands.name: diff
subcommands.description: Displays the differences between the dependencies of two distinct workspaces. If a single parameter is passed, the specified workspace's dependencies are compared with the master's. If no parameter is passed, the diff is made between the current workspace and master

args.name: WORKSPACE1
args.description: First workspace for comparison

args.name: WORKSPACE2
args.description: [default: master] Second workspace for comparison

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Displays the complete dependency tree of the current workspace

name: --npm
-n
options.description: Includes dependencies from the npm registry

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates a dependency of the current workspace. If not specified which dependency, it updates all of them

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to update

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to update

subcommands.name: edition
subcommands.description: Displays the Edition App version installed on the current account

subcommands.name: get
subcommands.description: Displays the Edition App version installed on the current account

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets the Edition App version for the current account

args.name: EDITION
args.description: Name of the Edition App to install

subcommands.name: help
subcommands.description: Displays help for VTEX CLI commands

name: --all
options.description: Displays all commands available in the CLI

args.name: COMMAND
args.description: Command to show help about

subcommands.name: infra
subcommands.description: Infra service

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs an infra service

args.name: SERVICEID
args.description: Name and version of the service ({vendor}.{servicename}@{x.x.x}) to install

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists installed infra services

name: --available
-a
options.description: Lists services that are available to install

name: --filter=filter
-f
options.description: Lists services that contain the specified word

args.name: NAME
args.description: Service name

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates all installed infra services

subcommands.name: init
subcommands.description: Copies starting files and folders from VTEX boilerplates into your local directories

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: Installs an app on the current workspace. If not specified which one, it defaults to the app in the current directory

name: --force
-f
options.description: Installs the specified app without checking for route conflicts

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to install

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to install

subcommands.name: lighthouse
subcommands.description: Runs a Lighthouse audit over the specified URL

subcommands.name: audit
subcommands.description: Runs a Lighthouse audit over the specified URL

name: --json
-j
options.description: Returns the report as a json on stdout

args.name: URL
args.description: URL to audit

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows a previous audit report, filtering by app and/or URL

subcommands.name: lh
subcommands.description: Runs a Lighthouse audit over the specified URL

subcommands.name: audit
subcommands.description: Runs a Lighthouse audit over the specified URL

name: --json
-j
options.description: Returns the report as a json on stdout

args.name: URL
args.description: URL to audit

subcommands.name: show
subcommands.description: Shows a previous audit report, filtering by app and/or URL

subcommands.name: link
subcommands.description: Syncs the app in the current directory with the development workspace in use

name: --account=account
-a
options.description: Starts a development session in the specified account. Must be paired with the '--workspace' flag

name: --setup
-s
options.description: Sets up typing definitions before linking the app [see vtex setup --help]

name: --unsafe
-u
options.description: Allows linking the app despite Typescript errors

name: --workspace=workspace
-w
options.description: Starts a development session in the specified workspace. Can be paired with the '--account' flag to switch from the current account and workspace

name: --no-watch
options.description: Doesn't watch for file changes after the initial link

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists the apps installed on the current workspace and account

subcommands.name: local token
subcommands.description: Prints the user's auth token and copies it to the clipboard

subcommands.name: login
subcommands.description: Login to a VTEX account

args.name: ACCOUNT
args.description: Account name to log in

subcommands.name: logout
subcommands.description: Logs out of the current VTEX account

subcommands.name: logs
subcommands.description: Shows logs of an app. (Only apps in production.)

name: --all
-a
options.description: Shows logs of every app installed on the current account

name: --past
-p
options.description: Shows previous logs of the specified app

args.name: APP
args.description: Name of the app to show logs

subcommands.name: publish
subcommands.description: Publishes the app in the current directory as a release candidate version

name: --force
-f
options.description: Publishes the app independently of SemVer rules

name: --tag=tag
-t
options.description: Adds the specified tag to the release

name: --workspace=workspace
-w
options.description: Uses the specified workspace in the app registry

subcommands.name: redirects
subcommands.description: Redirects from the current account and workspace

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes redirects from the current account and workspace

args.name: CSVPATH
args.description: CSV file containing the URL paths to be deleted

subcommands.name: export
subcommands.description: Exports all redirects defined in the current account and workspace to a CSV file

args.name: CSVPATH
args.description: Name of the CSV file

subcommands.name: import
subcommands.description: Imports redirects from a CSV file to the current account and workspace

subcommands.name: release
subcommands.description: (Only for git users.) Bumps the app version, commits, and pushes to remote the app in the current directory

args.name: RELEASETYPE
args.description: Release type (major, minor, or patch)

args.name: TAGNAME
args.description: Tag name (e.g., stable, beta)

subcommands.name: settings
subcommands.description: VTEX CLI Settings

args.name: APNAME
args.description: Name of the app to check the available settings

args.name: FIELD
args.description: Name of the setting

subcommands.name: set
subcommands.description: Sets value to the specified setting of an app

args.name: APPNAME
args.description: Name of the app

args.name: FIELD
args.description: Name of the setting

args.name: VALUE
args.description: Value of the setting

subcommands.name: unset
subcommands.description: Disables the specified setting of an app

args.name: APPNAME
args.description: Name of the app

args.name: FIELD
args.description: Name of the setting

subcommands.name: setup
subcommands.description: Sets up typings and tools for the current development environment

name: --ignore-linked
-i
options.description: Sets up types from published apps, and ignores types from linked apps

name: --all
options.description: Sets up all available typings, configs, and tools

name: --tooling
options.description: Sets up Prettier, Husky, and ESLint

name: --tsconfig
options.description: Sets up React and Node TSconfig, if applicable

name: --typings
options.description: Sets up GraphQL and React typings

subcommands.name: submit
subcommands.description: Submits the current app, or an specified one, to validation from VTEX App Store team

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name of the app to be validated

subcommands.name: support
subcommands.description: Logs in as support to another VTEX account

args.name: ACCOUNT
args.description: Name of the account to give support

subcommands.name: switch
subcommands.description: Switches to another VTEX account

args.name: ACCOUNT
args.description: Account name to log in

subcommands.name: test
subcommands.description: Runs unit tests for the app in the current directory

subcommands.name: e2e
subcommands.description: Runs E2E integration tests for the app in the current directory

name: --report=report
-r
options.description: Displays the results and state of the specified test ID

name: --token
-t
options.description: (Not recommended.) Sends your personal authorization token to your testing session, making it available during the tests. It can be dangerous since it exposes your token via the 'authToken' environment variable

name: --workspace
-w
options.description: Runs tests for the apps installed on the specified workspace

subcommands.name: unit
subcommands.description: Runs unit tests for the app in the current directory

subcommands.name: undeprecate
subcommands.description: Reestablishes a deprecated version of an app as a stable version

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to undeprecate

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to undeprecate

subcommands.name: uninstall
subcommands.description: Uninstalls an app from the current workspace. If not specified which app to uninstall, it defaults to the app in the current directory

args.name: APPNAME
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to uninstall

args.name: ITHAPPNAME
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to uninstall

subcommands.name: unlink
subcommands.description: Unlinks an app from the current workspace. If not specified which app to unlink, it defaults to the app in the current directory

args.name: APPID
args.description: Name and version of the app ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to unlink

args.name: ITHAPPID
args.description: Names and versions of the multiple apps ({vendor}.{appname}@{x.x.x}) to unlink

subcommands.name: update
subcommands.description: Updates all installed apps to the latest (minor or patch) version. Does not upgrade to another major version

subcommands.name: url
subcommands.description: Prints base URL for the current account and workspace

subcommands.name: whoami
subcommands.description: Prints the current account, workspace, environment, and login details

subcommands.name: workspace
subcommands.description: Workspace from the current account

subcommands.name: abtest
subcommands.description: A/B tests from running on the current account

subcommands.name: finish
subcommands.description: Stops all A/B tests from running on the current account

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: Starts a new A/B test on the current workspace

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Displays the results of the active A/B tests

subcommands.name: delete
subcommands.description: Deletes one or many workspaces from the current account

name: --force
-f
options.description: Deletes the specified workspace even if it is currently in use

args.name: WORKSPACE1
args.description: Name of the workspace to delete

args.name: ITHWORKSPACE
args.description: Name of the multiple workspaces to delete

subcommands.name: list
subcommands.description: Lists all workspaces of the current account

subcommands.name: promote
subcommands.description: Promotes the current workspace to master. Only works for production workspaces

subcommands.name: reset
subcommands.description: Cleans all configurations of the specified workspace and recreates it with the configurations from master

name: --production
-p
options.description: Recreates the workspace as a production one

args.name: WORKSPACENAME
args.description: Name of the workspace to reset

subcommands.name: status
subcommands.description: Displays information about the specified workspace

args.name: WORKSPACENAME
args.description: Name of the workspace

subcommands.name: use
subcommands.description: Creates and switches to a new workspace or simply switches to an existing one

name: --production
-p
options.description: Creates and/or switches to a production workspace

name: --reset
-r
options.description: Resets the workspace before switching to it

args.name: WORKSPACE
args.description: Name of the workspace to use
